title: Addressable structured items in descriptions, with qualified ids
description:
Generalize requirements from bespoke text-parsing into a first-class **addressable structured item** model: any structured line in an issue description (or linked plan doc) can carry a self-id and be addressed, indexed, searched, and linked by a globally-unique **qualified id**. Requirements are the first kind; the model is generic.

## Context

The validation engine is already generic over `(section, marker, id-pattern)` — `criterion_ids()` (`crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs`) and the `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check` / `criteria-label-match` rules all operate on that shape; "requirement" is just one configuration. Config already declares `[namespaces.*]` and `[type_hierarchy]` as registries. This issue names and consolidates that latent pattern rather than adding new surface area.

Today a requirement is a line of prose with a **locally-scoped** self-id (`REQ-01` is only unambiguous within one container's subtree), discoverable only by running validation. This makes such items central, addressable, and searchable.

## Concept

- **Addressable item** — a structured line in a declared section, carrying a self-id matched by an id-pattern, optionally a marker. Its **qualified id is `<issue-id>/<self-id>`** — globally unique, **derived, never stored twice**.
- **Item kind** — a config-declared projection `(section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s))`, parallel to `[namespaces.*]`. `requirement` = (`success_criteria`, `REQ-\d+`, `[hard]`, `satisfies:`).
- **Generic operations** — `jit item list/show/search [--kind …]`, resolve `jit show <issue>/<self-id>`, and generic link labels `<namespace>:<issue>/<self-id>` (`satisfies:`, `realizes:`, `mitigates:`, …).
- The existing coverage/check rules become **instances** over this model rather than bespoke logic.

## Principles

- **Single source of truth** — markdown stays authoritative; the item index and qualified ids are a pure projection. No hand-maintained structured duplicate (avoid the type-declaration triple-source trap).
- **Domain-agnostic** — item kinds are opt-in per project, declared in config; no kind name hardcoded in engine logic.
- **Graceful degradation** — a line with no self-id is plain prose and incurs no addressing requirement; projects pay for addressing only where they want it.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: Item kinds are declared in config as `(section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s))`; `requirement` is expressible as one such kind; no kind name is hardcoded in engine logic (domain-agnostic).
- [hard] REQ-02: Every addressable item has a globally-unique qualified id `<issue-id>/<self-id>`, derived from existing data (not separately stored); self-id uniqueness within an issue is validated and a stable self-id is required.
- [hard] REQ-03: Markdown remains the single source of truth — the item index is a pure projection with no hand-maintained structured copy.
- [hard] REQ-04: Items are queryable and searchable across the repo (e.g. `jit item list/show/search`, `--kind` filter) and resolvable by qualified id.
- [hard] REQ-05: Generic link namespaces reference qualified ids (`<ns>:<issue>/<self-id>`); the existing `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check` rules are re-expressed as instances over the model or shown compatible with it.
- [hard] REQ-06: Addressing degrades gracefully — a line without a self-id is plain prose and is not required to be addressable.

## Non-goals

- First-class per-item lifecycle/state records (L2) — deferred unless per-item state becomes a hard need.
- Cross-issue item→item dependency edges — enabled by global ids but out of scope here (future).
- Modifying `jit-plan` or other skills.

## Relationships and use cases

- **`jit-plan` (`eed6750c`) is a beneficiary, not a blocker.** It leverages this to emit a traceable graph — requirements, decisions, risks, decomposition-sketch tasks — but works at L0 without it. Sibling under v1.0, no dependency edge.
- Kinds beyond requirements that this immediately enables: **decisions/ADRs** (`D-NN`, `per:`), **risks / open questions** (`RISK-NN`/`OQ-NN`, `mitigates:`), **acceptance checks** (`CHK-NN`, `verify:` — already gestured at by `criteria-to-check`), **API/interface contracts** (`API-NN`), **scenarios / user journeys** (Given/When/Then), **invariants / NFRs**, **glossary terms** (drift detection), and **research targets** (`FIG-3`/`TBL-2` for a "reproduce the article" goal, with `reproduces:goal/FIG-3`). Software requirements and research targets are the same primitive — consistent with jit's domain-agnostic stance.
